name: Emedastine
description: Emedastine is an antihistamine used in eye drops to treat allergic conjunctivitis.
indication: For the temporary relief of the signs and symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis.
toxicity: Somnolence and malaise have been reported following daily oral administration.
mechanismOfAction: Emedastine is a relatively selective, histamine H<sub>1</sub> antagonist. In vitro examinations of emedastine's affinity for histamine receptors demonstrate relative selectivity for the H<sub>1</sub> histamine receptor. In vivo studies have shown concentration-dependent inhibition of histamine-stimulated vascular permeability in the conjunctiva following topical ocular administration. Emedastine appears to be devoid of effects on adrenergic, dopaminergic and serotonin receptors.
pharmacodynamics: Emedastine is a relatively selective H<sub>1</sub>-receptor antagonist.

interactions.name: Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine
interactions.description: Emedastine may decrease effectiveness of Benzylpenicilloyl Polylysine as a diagnostic agent.

interactions.name: Hyaluronidase
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Hyaluronidase can be decreased when used in combination with Emedastine.

interactions.name: Betahistine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Betahistine can be decreased when used in combination with Emedastine.

interactions.name: Pseudoephedrine
interactions.description: Pseudoephedrine may decrease the sedative activities of Emedastine.

interactions.description: Diethylpropion may decrease the sedative activities of Emedastine.
